segments.text: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Should not the multitude of words be answered? And should a man full of talk be vindicated?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Should your empty talk make men hold their peace? And when you mock, should no one rebuke you?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For you have said,
My doctrine is pure, And I am clean in your eyes.
segments.speaker: Job (quoted by Zophar)

segments.text: But oh, that God would speak, And open His lips against you,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: That He would show you the secrets of wisdom! For they would double your prudence. Know therefore that God exacts from you Less than your iniquity deserves.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Can you search out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limits of the Almighty?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: They are higher than heaven—what can you do? Deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Their measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If He passes by, imprisons, and gathers to judgment, Then who can hinder Him?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For He knows deceitful men; He sees wickedness also. Will He not then consider it?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For an empty-headed man will be wise, When a wild donkey's colt is born a man.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If you would prepare your heart, And stretch out your hands toward Him;
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If iniquity were in your hand, and you put it far away, And would not let wickedness dwell in your tents;
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Then surely you could lift up your face without spot; Yes, you could be steadfast, and not fear;
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Because you would forget your misery, And remember it as waters that have passed away,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: And your life would be brighter than noonday. Though you were dark, you would be like the morning.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: And you would be secure, because there is hope; Yes, you would dig around you, and take your rest in safety.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: You would also lie down, and no one would make you afraid; Yes, many would court your favor.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: But the eyes of the wicked will fail, And they shall not escape, And their hope—loss of life!
segments.speaker: Zophar
